article_title: Update to guided tours and sampling at a manufacturer site
body: These changes apply to the following licences: Maximum individual sample sizes have been removed from theManufacturer Licence Terms and Conditions.Instead, there are new and increased limits on the volume of samples that may besoldto each patron per day: There are no limits on the volume offree samplesyou can serve patrons daily, but you must not serve patrons to the point of intoxication. The maximum volume of samples sold per patron per day applies even if the samples are served in multiple areas. Manufacturers may vary guided tour routes anywhere on the manufacturing site. Seated intervals are permitted during a guided tour and must be supervised by the tour guide. Temporary seating provided for patrons during a tour is not intended to be an alternate consumption area. Packaged liquor sales from an on-site store are now permitted on a guided tour. For example, through a mobile-point-of-sale system. Guided tours cannot pass through other businesses located onsite or service areas under a separate liquor licence unless they are held by the manufacturer. To read all the rules for these changes, please refer to the: Bulletins are for general information and may not apply to all situations. Bulletins do not constitute legal advice nor are they a comprehensive statement of the legal obligations that arise under the Liquor Control and Licensing Act, Cannabis Control and Licensing Act, regulations, or any other applicable laws. When interpreting and applying the information contained in the Bulletins, you are encouraged to seek advice from your professional advisors as appropriate in the circumstances.

minutes_briefs.title: BC liquor manufacturers: new rules for tour samples and sales
minutes_briefs.summary: BC has updated the rules for liquor manufacturers who offer guided tours and samples. The old limits on individual sample sizes are gone. Instead, there are new daily limits on how much liquor can be sold as samples to each patron. Free samples have no volume limit, but you cannot serve anyone to the point of intoxication. Manufacturers can now change tour routes anywhere on site, allow seated breaks during tours, and sell packaged liquor from an on-site store during a tour using a mobile payment system. Tours cannot go through other businesses or areas under a separate liquor licence unless the manufacturer holds that licence too.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review your tour and sampling procedures to comply with the new daily sold-sample limits and tour route rules.

The maximum volume of samples sold per patron per day applies even if the samples are served in multiple areas. Manufacturers may vary guided tour routes anywhere on the manufacturing site. Seated intervals are permitted during a guided tour and must be supervised by the tour guide. Temporary seating provided for patrons during a tour is not intended to be an alternate consumption area. Packaged liquor sales from an on-site store are now permitted on a guided tour. For example, through a mobile-point-of-sale system. Guided tours cannot pass through other businesses located onsite or service areas under a separate liquor licence unless they are held by the manufacturer. To read all the rules for these changes, please refer to the: Bulletins are for general information and may not apply to all situations. Bulletins do not constitute legal advice nor are they a comprehensive statement of the legal obligations that arise under the Liquor Control and Licensing Act, Cannabis Control and Licensing Act, regulations, or any other applicable laws.
